risk: A file editor can destroy or normalize user Engine.ini content
mitigation: Lossless merge, external-change guard, byte backup, atomic replace, readback hash verification, failure tests

risk: High or Realtime priority can reduce system responsiveness
mitigation: Normal default, never auto-select dangerous levels, persistent accessible warnings, explicit user action, readback status

risk: Updater signing key loss or exposure breaks the update trust chain
mitigation: Protected secrets, encrypted offline recovery, draft release, mandatory signature and tamper tests

risk: Game or account action may result from an unofficial third-party tool
mitigation: No injection/hooks/bypass, current terms link, explicit bilingual acknowledgement, no safety guarantee, seek written Kuro guidance before broad promotion

risk: A Kuro character-derived app icon may fall outside fan-work permission for software
mitigation: Use a completely original geometric T monogram and exclude generated character art from the repository and release

risk: Community Engine.ini claims may be ignored or regress after patches
mitigation: Evidence states, tested-version metadata, promotion protocol, no automatic reapply after external/game patch change

risk: macOS development host cannot directly verify Windows APIs and NSIS runtime
mitigation: Windows GitHub Actions, helper-process integration tests, clean Windows VM release gate

risk: A global background-priority mode can cause audio dropouts, recording loss, input lag, application instability, or restoration of the wrong PID
mitigation: Default off; preview and explicit confirmation; same-user/session eligibility; critical/foreground/audio/communication/recording exclusions; PID+creation-time+image+applied-value restore guard; durable journal; no persistent registry or power-plan changes
